title: CSS Box Shadow Generator
description: Generate CSS box-shadow code with live preview. Adjust offset, blur, spread, color, and opacity — copy with one click.

howToUse.label: Adjust the parameters
howToUse.description: Use the sliders or input fields to set the X/Y offset, blur, and spread. You can also customize the shadow color and opacity.

howToUse.label: Check the live preview
howToUse.description: The preview updates in real time as you adjust parameters. Fine-tune until the shadow looks exactly how you want.

howToUse.label: Copy the CSS code
howToUse.description: Click 'copy' to copy the box-shadow value to your clipboard. Paste it directly into your stylesheet.

faq.question: What does inset do?
faq.answer: Normally, shadows are drawn outside the element. With 'inset', the shadow appears inside the element's border. It's commonly used for pressed-button effects or to add depth to form inputs.

faq.question: What's the difference between blur and spread?
faq.answer: Blur radius controls how soft the shadow edge is (0 = sharp). Spread radius expands or shrinks the shadow size (positive = larger, negative = smaller) before blur is applied.

faq.question: Can I layer multiple shadows?
faq.answer: Yes — CSS allows multiple shadows separated by commas, e.g. `box-shadow: 2px 2px 4px red, -2px -2px 4px blue`. This tool generates one shadow at a time, but you can combine multiple generated values with a comma.

faq.question: What do the values in the generated code mean?
faq.answer: The format is: `[inset] offsetX offsetY blur spread color`. For example: `4px 6px 8px 0px rgba(0,0,0,0.2)` means 4px right, 6px down, 8px blur, no spread, 20% black. Inset comes first if enabled.
